data_in.description: airway SummarizedExperiment object from Bioconductor airway package

data_out.description: Results table with columns: baseMean, log2FoldChange, lfcSE, stat, pvalue, padj for treated-vs-untreated contrast
Count of genes with adjusted p-value < 0.1

domain_knowledge: DESeq2 expects un-normalized integer counts (not normalized values) as input to ensure correct assessment of measurement precision via the negative binomial model.

The design formula must be specified before running DESeq(); for the airway dataset the formula ~cell+dex indicates cell type is a blocking factor and dex (dexamethasone treatment) is the variable of interest.

By default, results() performs automatic independent filtering on mean normalized counts to optimize power for FDR control, and the alpha parameter (default 0.1) sets the target FDR level for this filtering.

Factor levels are ordered alphabetically by default in R; the reference level for contrasts must be explicitly set or the results() contrast argument used to specify the numerator and denominator groups.
Adjusted p-values (padj) account for multiple testing via the Benjamini-Hochberg method and are the appropriate threshold for significance claims in differential expression studies.

direct_checks: verify file exists: airway dataset loaded from airway R package via library('airway'); data('airway')

script_runs: DESeq2 pipeline executes without error on airway dataset with commands: dds <- DESeqDataSet(airway); dds <- DESeq(dds); res <- results(dds, contrast=c('condition','treated','untreated'), alpha=0.1)
value_in_range: number of genes with padj < 0.1 is a non-negative integer

output_matches_reference: count of significant genes (padj < 0.1) from treated-vs-untreated contrast reproducible across independent runs with identical parameter settings (robust to random seed where applicable)
expert_review: statistical validity: verify that DESeq2 was called with correct default parameters (negative binomial GLM, standard dispersion estimation, independent filtering enabled by default in results() function)
contrast specification: confirm treated-vs-untreated contrast is correctly specified and that reference level ordering matches reported analysis

FDR control: verify that alpha=0.1 threshold is applied to adjusted p-values (padj) rather than raw p-values, and that independent filtering does not artificially inflate or suppress significant gene count

cmd: library(DESeq2); library(airway); data(airway); dds <- DESeqDataSet(airway, design = ~cell + dex); dds <- DESeq(dds); res <- results(dds, alpha=0.1); write.csv(as.data.frame(res), 'results_table.csv'); cat('Count of genes with padj < 0.1:', sum(res$padj < 0.1, na.rm=TRUE), '\n')

finding: The airway dataset is available in Bioconductor for use with DESeq2 differential expression analysis of treated versus untreated samples.
inputs: airway SummarizedExperiment object from Bioconductor airway package
methodology_summary: Load airway SummarizedExperiment and construct DESeqDataSet with design ~cell+dex to account for cell type variation while testing dexamethasone treatment effect.
Estimate per-gene dispersions using the negative binomial GLM framework, shrinking estimates toward a mean-dispersion trend fitted across all genes.
Fit negative binomial GLM for each gene and compute Wald test statistics and p-values for the treatment coefficient.
Apply automatic independent filtering based on mean normalized count to optimize the number of genes achieving padj < alpha, where alpha=0.1 (default FDR target).
Extract results table with log2 fold changes, standard errors, test statistics, raw p-values, and Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted p-values for treated-vs-untreated contrast.

Validation: verify that the count of genes with padj < 0.1 matches the reported value from the vignette and that results table structure includes all required columns (baseMean, log2FoldChange, lfcSE, stat, pvalue, padj).

task_description: Run DESeq2's standard differential expression pipeline on the airway dataset to test for treated-vs-untreated differences, applying the default FDR threshold (alpha=0.1), and produce a results table with log2 fold changes and adjusted p-values.
task_objective: Run DESeq2 differential expression pipeline on the airway dataset and report the count of genes with adjusted p-value < 0.1 in the treated-vs-untreated contrast.

task_outputs.label: DESeq2 results table with fold changes and p-values

title: Reproduce the count of genes with adjusted p-value < 0.1 in the airway dataset DESeq2 analysis

workflow_description: 1. Load the airway SummarizedExperiment dataset and construct a DESeqDataSet with design formula ~cell+dex. 2. Run DESeq() to estimate dispersions and fit negative binomial generalized linear models for each gene. 3. Extract results using results(dds) with default alpha=0.1, which automatically performs independent filtering based on mean normalized counts. 4. Order the results table by adjusted p-value to identify significantly differentially expressed genes (padj < 0.1).
